suggestions.title: R-key refresh does not clear paused polling state
suggestions.description: The keyboard shortcut handler for 'r'/'R' calls `syncFromGitHub()` directly instead of `onResume()`. When the user is in the paused-due-to-inactivity state and presses R to refresh, the data fetches correctly but `pollingPaused` stays true, `lastActivity.current` is not reset, and the ⏸ Paused banner remains visible indefinitely. Fix: replace `syncFromGitHub()` with `onResume()` in the keydown handler branch for R/r.

suggestions.title: Workers transitioning to 'done' status disappear silently with no celebration
suggestions.description: The `justShipped` detection only catches `working → idle` transitions. A worker whose status goes directly to 'done' is filtered out of `activeWorkers` (which only includes working/idle/error), triggering the plain fade-out animation with zero confetti or completion toast. STATUS_LABELS and STATUS_EMOJIS both define 'done', meaning it was designed to be shown, but no render path ever displays it. Either extend `activeWorkers` to include 'done' workers temporarily, or detect `working→done` the same way `working→idle` is detected and fire the celebration.

suggestions.title: Mobile: pinned hover card on Office characters has no visible dismiss control
suggestions.description: On touch devices, tapping a character in the Office view toggles `pinnedCard` instead of navigating to dashboard (correct behavior per `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)')`). But the resulting `char-hover-card` popup has no close button — the only way to dismiss it is to tap the same character again, which is non-obvious. Add a small ✕ button or an outside-tap-to-dismiss handler (a transparent overlay or document click listener) to the pinned card path.

suggestions.title: Footer quote counter disappears on the last item in the cycle
suggestions.description: The counter `{quoteBonus}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length}` only renders when `quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length`. So the 8th (last) quote shows no counter, silently hiding the position indicator right when users most need to know they've reached the end. One line fix: change the condition from `quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length` to `quoteBonus <= BONUS_TAGLINES.length`.

suggestions.title: Worker cards are not keyboard-navigable via Tab
suggestions.description: `WorkerCard` has `tabIndex={-1}` on the outer `role="article"` wrapper, meaning keyboard-only users cannot Tab between cards in the Dashboard grid. The ℹ️ flip button and task tags inside are focusable, but users must already be inside the card to reach them. Consider `tabIndex={0}` on the card wrapper, or at minimum ensure the ℹ️ button is reachable from outside the card. The current behavior means screen reader users get read-only summary only via the `aria-label` but cannot interact without a mouse.

suggestions.title: Office.css is 2411 lines — split into feature-scoped modules
suggestions.description: Office.css has grown to 2411 lines covering desks, characters, avatars, conference table, wall clock, whiteboard, weather, plants, door, and animations. This is a maintenance liability: any targeted change risks unintended side effects and the file is hard to search. Recommend splitting into colocated CSS modules (e.g. Character.module.css, Desk.module.css, OfficeDecorations.module.css) or at minimum logical @layer sections with clear demarcation comments. App.css at 1170 lines is approaching the same problem.

suggestions.title: isTouchDevice evaluated at module load — breaks hybrid devices
suggestions.description: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is evaluated once when the module is imported. On hybrid laptops/tablets that support both mouse and touch, users who switch input modes mid-session will see stale messaging (e.g. the Paused banner still says 'Move mouse to refresh' after they switch to touch). Move this into a `useEffect` or `useCallback` that reads `window.matchMedia` at call time rather than at import time.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime shows 'just now' for future-dated timestamps
suggestions.description: `getRelativeTime` in utils/time.js computes `diff = Date.now() - new Date(timestamp).getTime()`. If the activity.json server clock is ahead of the client (clock skew) or an entry has a future timestamp, diff is negative, `mins < 1` is true, and the function returns 'just now'. This could show stale or future activity as 'just now'. Add a guard: `if (diff < 0) return 'just now'` is already effectively there, but consider returning a neutral 'moments ago' or clamping to 0 explicitly so the intent is clear to future maintainers.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary retry button uses no design system styles
suggestions.description: The ErrorBoundary's catch UI renders a plain `<button>` with only `style={{ marginLeft: '1rem', cursor: 'pointer' }}`. It completely breaks visual consistency — no background, no border-radius, no color tokens. This is a rare state but users who hit it deserve the same polish as the rest of the app. Apply `.btn btn-sm btn-ghost` or an equivalent class consistent with the retry buttons elsewhere in the app.

suggestions.title: Vibe history written to localStorage on every teamVibe.key change — including initial mount
suggestions.description: The vibe-history effect runs on every `teamVibe.key` change, reads the full history JSON from localStorage, appends, and writes it back. On fast polling cycles where vibe flickers (e.g. single worker toggling in/out), this could generate several localStorage writes per 30-second interval. Low risk currently with only 5 workers, but consider debouncing the write or only writing when the key genuinely changes (guard: `if (prevKey === teamVibe.key) return` before the write).

praise: The 19-entry VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES map is absurdly thorough — every vibe-to-vibe transition has a bespoke Bloberto one-liner. This is the kind of detail that makes the app feel alive.
Worker-specific desk screen SVGs with separate error variants (carlos_error, sofia_error, etc.) are a delightful touch that most teams would never bother with.
safeSave/safeRead utility properly handles iOS private browsing and enterprise localStorage restrictions — quiet defensive coding that will save someone's sanity eventually.
The keyboard shortcut system is genuinely polished: ShortcutToast auto-dismisses, shows 'Got it ✓' confirmation, and persists dismissal in localStorage so it never nags.
PING_REACTIONS gives each worker a distinct voice (Carlos will always reference logs, Sofia will always reference edge cases). This is premium character work.
The dynamic favicon (red/green/grey canvas circle) is a small thing that shows real attention to the full browser experience, not just the viewport.
The paused polling state correctly differentiates touch vs mouse messaging — 'Tap to refresh' vs 'Move mouse to refresh' — a genuinely thoughtful UX detail.
summary: Review #87 finds the app in excellent aesthetic shape — visual polish and fun factor remain at full marks, and the level of characterization (worker-specific SVGs, ping reactions, vibe transition quotes) continues to be genuinely impressive. The score holds steady at 4.1 overall. The one concrete bug worth immediate attention is the R-key shortcut bypassing `onResume()`, which leaves the Paused banner stuck on screen after a manual refresh — a small inconsistency that undermines the otherwise solid keyboard UX. The silent disappearance of workers who transition to 'done' (no confetti, no toast) is a missed celebration moment that the animation infrastructure already supports. Mobile users are still slightly disadvantaged: the pinned hover card has no obvious dismiss path, and at 375px the office floor text labels (0.38rem nameplates) are approaching unreadable territory. Code quality is holding but the CSS files are inching toward 'nobody wants to touch this' territory — Office.css at 2411 lines is a ticking time bomb for the next person who needs to tweak desk layout on a specific breakpoint.
